rulings.Case Title: BRUCE WAYGOOD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes Including Trial
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 20, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff's motion for order to consolidate actions for all purposes including trial is granted. No opposition filed. Cases numbered 274871 and 275895 are consolidated. Henceforth all papers shall be filed under the lead case no. 274871. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MILAN BACOKA et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 20, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant FMC Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Mr. Bacoka's deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Nykorowytsch's deposition testimony and defendant's responses to General Order No. 129 standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Bacoka was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (OEM packing) attributable to defendant. Defendant's moving papers failed to properly make substantial factor causation an issue. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation and civil battery causes of action and claim for punitive damages, which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 20, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Dowman Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CINDY LINDLEY et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 20, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its burden of production. Plaintiffs submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's objections to Mr. Crum's deposition testimony are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Crosby's deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: VICKY WOOLLEY et al VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Dowman Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Considered together, Mr. Rayburn¿s declaration, Lindy Schluter¿s declaration, Mr. Lewis¿ deposition and trial testimony, Mr. Cook¿s declaration, Mr. Wellington¿s deposition and trial testimony and Mr. Robert Dowds¿ deposition testimony create a triable issue whether Aline Ivance was exposed to asbestos-containing FixAll manufactured by defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS et al VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7, Defendant Cosco Fire Protection, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and motion for summary adjudication are denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. 437c(a). The proof of service filed with defendant¿s moving papers demonstrates that defendant did not serve the moving papers until February 6, 2012, which did not provide proper notice with a March 20, 2012 hearing date. Plaintiffs¿ objection based on untimeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MCHUGH et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 8, Defendant LVI Environmental Services, Inc.¿s motion for judgment on the pleadings as to plaintiffs¿ fourth (loss of consortium) and fifth (premises owner/contractor liability) causes of action is granted with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 20, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 9, Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is denied. Plaintiffs¿ complaint ¿ the summary complaint and Brayton Purcell¿s master complaint ¿ states allegations sufficient to properly plead a claim for punitive damages. The court did not consider the deposition testimony of Darryl Diamond, which both parties attached. The grounds for a motion to strike must appear either on the face of the challenged pleading or from matters which the court may judicially notice. (C.C.P. § 437.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email tocontestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
